calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-20-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summ Adjud / Notice
Off Calendar Oct-21-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL A. ROBINSON APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAY C. PATTERSON OF CODDINGTON, HICKS & DANFORTH LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. (650) 592-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA AND UTAH INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Defendant has established that Plaintiff tendered the defense of the wrongful death action to Defendant and Defendant provided a defense in that case. (UMF #4.) Plaintiff may not rely on allegations in the Complaint to controvert material facts asserted by Defendant, and Plaintiff failed to produce any admissible evidence to controvert this fact. The statements cited in Plaintiff's declaration are inadmissible hearsay. Next, Defendant has also shown that the conveyance action was solely equitable in nature and that no damages for bodily injury or property damage were sought or could have been recoverable. (UMF #6.) Plaintiff's reliance on the request for "general and special damages" in the Complaint is not a material fact because such remedies are unavailable for the causes of action pled. Thus, Plaintiff has failed to controvert Defendant's evidence and create a triable issue of material fact. Defendant is also entitled to judgment in its favor for the sixth cause of action for breach of contract because Plaintiff failed to produce any admissible evidence establishing a triable issue as to Defendant's liability under the alleged agreements. Finally, because Plaintiff has produced no admissible evidence to support his claim that this breach was the result of Defendant's failure to provide an adequate defense in the wrongful death action, Plaintiff has failed to controvert Defendant's asserted material fact that the cause of action for breach of good faith was premised on the conveyance action. (UMF #10.) Because Defendant did not have a duty to defend Plaintiff in that action, Defendant necessarily cannot be liable for breach of good faith as alleged by Plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
